evals.expected_output: Should identify multiple smells (deep nesting, long parameter list, duplicated formatting logic, SRP violation), check for tests first, then apply transformations one at a time.
assertions: Asks about or checks for existing tests before refactoring
Identifies deep nesting as the primary smell
Identifies duplicated format logic between user and order branches
Applies early returns to flatten nesting
Extracts formatting logic into a shared function
Introduces a parameter object or options pattern
Runs or suggests running tests between steps

evals.prompt: I want to refactor this module but there are no tests. What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Should insist on writing characterization tests first, explain why, and help write them before any refactoring begins.
assertions: Does not start refactoring without tests
Explains characterization tests and their purpose
Offers to help write the characterization tests
Tests document current behavior, including quirks

evals.prompt: Can you refactor my entire codebase to use better patterns?
evals.expected_output: Should push back on the scope — suggest identifying the highest-impact areas first, then refactoring incrementally. Should not attempt a sweeping rewrite.
assertions: Does not attempt to refactor everything at once
Asks which areas cause the most pain or confusion
Applies KISS to the refactoring process itself
